items.headline: U.S. Markets Close Higher Ahead of Fed Decision and Hotter Inflation Data
items.summary: Major U.S. indices finished in positive territory on Wednesday, March 18, 2026, as investors weighed hotter-than-expected Producer Price Index data against anticipation for the Federal Reserve's policy announcement. All 11 sectors of the S&P 500 advanced, with technology and consumer discretionary leading the gains. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continued to keep crude oil prices elevated, adding to inflation concerns. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Western Digital Surges on Tech Strength
items.summary: Western Digital Corp. ($WDC) saw its stock price jump significantly, attributed to a strong performance by technology bigwigs.

items.headline: Macy's Beats Q4 Earnings Expectations
items.summary: Macy's ($M) shares rose after the company reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter earnings, beating both EPS and revenue forecasts.

items.headline: Micron Rises Ahead of Earnings Report
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) saw its stock rising ahead of its fiscal Q2 earnings report, with strong gains anticipated.

items.headline: SailPoint Slips Ahead of Earnings
items.summary: SailPoint ($SAIL) experienced a stock-specific decline ahead of its fiscal fourth-quarter and full-year 2026 earnings release.

items.headline: General Mills Misses Q3 Expectations
items.summary: General Mills ($GIS) shares fell in pre-market trading after the company missed fiscal Q3 earnings expectations.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Key Policy Meeting, Decision Expected
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) concluded its two-day meeting today, March 18, 2026, with Chairman Jerome Powell expected to deliver the policy statement and hold a press conference. Market expectations are for the federal funds rate to remain unchanged at 3.50-3.75%.

items.headline: Producer Price Index Surges, Fueling Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Producer Price Index (PPI) for February increased by 0.7%, more than double the estimated 0.3%. Year-over-year, PPI rose 3.4%, exceeding forecasts. This hotter-than-expected inflation data comes as the Federal Reserve prepares its policy announcement, potentially impacting the timeline for future rate cuts.

items.headline: S&P 500 Sectors Exhibit Broad-Based Gains
items.summary: All eleven sectors within the S&P 500 index finished today's trading session in positive territory. The Information Technology Select Sector SPDR ($XLK) and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ($XLY) were among the leading sectors, advancing 1.5% and 1.2% respectively, indicating a widespread positive sentiment across the market.

items.headline: Elevated Oil Prices Persist Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Crude oil prices remained high on Wednesday due to ongoing geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) futures rose to $96.21 per barrel, and Brent crude futures settled at $103.42 per barrel, contributing to persistent inflation concerns.
